metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A lich undead city where Lich Reliquary Tower of the Quiet Crown, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Oss Veyr should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Oss Veyr organizes power through Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, public response through Black Market Arcade Beacon, civic memory through Lich Reliquary Tower of the Quiet Crown, and daily life through S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Prison / Containment Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Limestone Blocks, Stone, Black Marble, Arcane Glow, Silver Inlay, Subterranean, Undercity Foundation, Limestone Caverns, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Necromantic Preservation, and exact anchors including Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Lich Reliquary Tower of the Quiet Crown, Alchemy Lab, Ancestor Vault, Containment Keep, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A lich undead civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Lich Reliquary Tower of the Quiet Crown, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, and section anchors such as Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Oss Veyr uses city code OVR.
The Wonder is Lich Reliquary Tower of the Quiet Crown.
The Beacon is Black Market Arcade Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Prison / Containment Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Oss Veyr as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Oss Veyr's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

civilization.people: The population should read as lich undead and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as Ancestor Vault, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Masks Monument.

bodyLanguage: workers move with practiced familiarity around district anchors

speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors

streets.appearance: Streets should show Oss Veyr's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Prison / Containment Civic Hall, S5 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
